current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Run a gated proxy recovery pass at `K=128` with the current best signum setup and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` to recheck stability on the strongest working family.
Run a gated proxy comparison at `K=32` with the recovered aux-free signum recipe to test whether the stable K=128/K=64 regime also improves the remaining lower-K frontier point.
Run a gated proxy comparison at `K=32` with the recovered aux-free signum recipe to test whether the stable K=128/K=64 regime also improves the remaining lower-K frontier point. full

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: `residual_topk` still has no quality signal because the latest prototype attempt failed at the same architecture-selection gate before training began. The smallest informative move remains a narrow code-fix round inside `sparsify/` that explicitly registers `residual_topk` in config validation and SAE class dispatch, so the intended K=128 proxy prototype can finally clear selection and reach a real sanity/training signal.

Crash recovery mode requires returning to the strongest working gated path and making only a minor parameter adjustment. The least-risk informative move is to keep the K=128 gated signum anchor fixed and restore a small auxiliary loss with `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` to check whether the canonical regularized regime improves training stability or quality without reopening architecture risk.

The standard gated hyperparameter sweeps at K=128 are exhausted and the recent `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` recovery check regressed, so the cleanest exploitation move is to reuse the best validated aux-free gated regime and probe the remaining adjacent lower-K point. Changing only K from the strongest K=128 anchor to K=32 tests whether the recovered gated recipe can strengthen the low-cost frontier without reopening blocked architecture paths.

recent_performance_findings: round 41: The recent `first_step_timeout` crashes are not specific to `group_topk`: the gated AUXK=0 recovery run also advanced through the first few microbatches and then stalled at the first optimizer step. That points to a shared training-path bottleneck rather than an architecture verdict. Replacing CUDA's per-step fired-latent `cat`+advanced-index update with an incremental boolean fired mask in `sparsify/trainer.py` should remove that first-step spike and let the unresolved `group_topk` K=128 proxy run complete under the current strongest regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=sanity_failed)

round 46: The obvious gated one-parameter sweep is now exhausted after clean regressions on both lower LR and lower expansion, so the next least-bad informative move is to unblock the incubating `factorized_topk` family rather than introduce another fresh direction. Its only evidence so far is a `sanity_failed` prototype, which should be treated as an architecture-compatibility issue, not a quality result. A narrow code fix inside `sparsify/` to make `factorized_topk` forward/backward compatible with the runtime should recover one valid proxy measurement at K=128 under the existing strongest regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=sanity_failed)

round 47: The straightforward gated hyperparameter sweep has now produced only clean regressions, so the next informative move is to recover the blocked incubating family rather than spend another round on weak local tuning. `factorized_topk` still has no usable quality signal because its only attempt failed at sanity. A narrow architecture-compatibility fix inside `sparsify/` should let the same K=128 proxy prototype run and finally reveal whether extra encoder mixing can improve over plain top-k. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=, k=None, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 49: `factorized_topk` has now failed for a concrete architecture-registration reason rather than an ambiguous quality or runtime signal: the execution layer rejects the architecture name in `SparseCoderConfig` before training begins. The next coherent move is a narrow code-fix round that wires `factorized_topk` into the existing config/class registry and forward path so the prototype can finally receive one real K=128 proxy measurement under the strongest current regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=, k=None, health=crash, termination=completed)

recent_sanity_failures.hypothesis: The obvious gated one-parameter sweep is now exhausted after clean regressions on both lower LR and lower expansion, so the next least-bad informative move is to unblock the incubating `factorized_topk` family rather than introduce another fresh direction. Its only evidence so far is a `sanity_failed` prototype, which should be treated as an architecture-compatibility issue, not a quality result. A narrow code fix inside `sparsify/` to make `factorized_topk` forward/backward compatible with the runtime should recover one valid proxy measurement at K=128 under the existing strongest regime.

recent_training_failures.hypothesis: Five rounds of factorized-topk stabilization failed to approach the gated frontier, so that direction is now effectively exhausted rather than merely under-tuned. The next mandatory informative move is a fundamentally different architecture family: a `residual_topk` SAE that spends the same total K budget across two sequential sparse stages, letting a second sparse pass model the first-pass residual instead of forcing one flat support set to capture everything at once.

next_move_guidance: If gated K=32 with `AUXK_ALPHA=0` improves over the current K=32 point, keep the aux-free regime alive across the low-K frontier.
If K=32 regresses, treat `AUXK_ALPHA=0` as beneficial mainly for K=64/128 and keep the canonical K=32 recipe when needed.
If the low-K gated sweep is exhausted without meaningful gains, shift the next round back toward architecture exploration once crash pressure is no longer dominant.
